buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df = df.sort_values(['asset', 'date'])

# Rolling mean per asset using only past data
df['rolling_mean'] = (
    df.groupby('asset')['returns']
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(window=60, min_periods=20).mean())
)

# Shift by 1 to avoid lookahead when using as feature
df['rolling_mean'] = df.groupby('asset')['rolling_mean'].shift(1)

# Drop NaN from rolling window warmup
df = df.dropna(subset=['rolling_mean'])

print(df.head())
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df = df.sort_values(['asset', 'date'])

df['rolling_mean'] = (
    df.groupby('asset')['returns']
    .transform(lambda x: x.rolling(window=60, min_periods=20).mean())
)

df['rolling_mean'] = df.groupby('asset')['rolling_mean'].shift(1)

df = df.dropna(subset=['rolling_mean'])

print(df.head())
description: Clean example — per-asset rolling mean using groupby transform, then shifted by 1.
explanation: rolling() inside transform computes per-asset windowed mean using only past data. Shifted by 1 after computation to avoid lookahead when used as a feature. min_periods=20 prevents noisy early values.
